observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to strictly monitor the disbursements from the LDRRMF to avoid transactions that are not related to the LDRRM Fund. We also recommended that the LCE direct the LGU Driver and all personnel concerned to properly fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket to aid the GSO and the Municipal Accountant in the fuel consumption monitoring of the LGU. Further, we recommended that management submit a justification explaining the deficiencies shown in Table 13.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Officer manifested that questions on the legality and validity of charging of these expenses was raised to the Finance Committee and was (Based on post-audit of CY 2022 utilization of DRRM Fund, transactions that are not related to the DRRM Fund, hence, considered as allowable charges to the fund.) The LDRRMF to the fund.) Further, it was asserted that it was due to financial constraints of the Municipality that prompted the allowance of these transactions as pertaining to and related to the LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Direct the former and the incumbent Barangay Chairperson and the Barangay Treasurer to locate the transaction documents related to the purpose of the grant and to seek alternative remedies to be able to liquidate and strictly implement proper turnover of the accounts and documents of the barangay, inasmuch as the transactions related to the outstanding unliquidated fund transfers are concerned, and impose appropriate administrative and legal action for refusal to do so; and,
b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor and strictly implement the rules underlying all financial assistance granted to the barangay in 2021 and the years thereafter as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the stipulation of the MOA.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already issued demand letters to these barangays but of no avail. The other barangays concerned are yet to submit liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer compute the area of the road
lots for the entire road network system of the Municipality and provide the same
to the Municipal Assessor for the latters use in the valuation of said lots.
Furthermore, to submit the results of road lot valuation to the Municipal
Accountant for recording in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant presents in the Notes to
Financial Statements the required disclosures for Property, Plant and
Equipment as prescribed in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the:
a. Supply Officer and Inspection Committee, properly accomplish the
Inspection and Acceptance Report every time goods are received; and
b. Municipal Accountant recognize accounts payable for the unpaid
purchases with properly accomplished IAR and other corroborating
supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer review and reconcile their respective records and registries and make
the necessary adjustment to correct the errors.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer regularly monitor all infrastructure projects to
avoid delayed implementations; and,
b. Municipal Accountant provides the computation of the liquidated
damages for deduction from the contractors claim.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned
department heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned
and effectively utilized to cater the development needs of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office provide the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) copies of the Drawings, Bill of Quantities, and other
detailed engineering documents, as part of the Philippine Bidding Documents,
for all infrastructure projects, regardless of the amount of the Approved Budget
for the Contract (ABC).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Vice-Mayor and the Human
Resources Management Officer to:
a. Review the support staff functions requirement per office to
determine the appropriate manpower complement of said office and
exercise prudence in the hiring of JO workers;
b. Ensure compliance with the hiring process and required documents
by the HRMO, before JO employees officially report to work; and
c. Stop assigning to the barangay, JO personnel which will perform
work and duties redundant with those of the existing Barangay
Officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Compute for the depreciated replacement cost of the spare parts replaced as a result of the major repair of the ambulance; and
b. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to assess the value of works associated to the minor repairs included in the contract and the cost of restoration of majorly damaged cottages and furnish the Municipal Accountant the results of the same; and,
b. The Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entries to correct the foregoing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Head of the Rural Health Unit and the LDRRMO to maintain records and monitor the receipts and issuances of all supplies and properties issued to the unit/office, and, regularly consolidate the consumption and issuances of these items for the use of the unit;
b. GSO to conduct semestral Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and compare this Report to the records of consolidated issuances of the department heads for reconciliation; and,
c. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with the RPCI as basis in recognizing related expenses.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer to determine with reasonable certainty – based on records and documents – the identification and budget allocation for priority development projects to ensure that the 20% development fund is fully optimized in implementing all planned projects that the fund could possibly finance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office in its preparation of the required PPMP, as supported by approved Program of Works, to include the infrastructure projects to which the office is tasked to implement to facilitate the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan in order that procurements are aligned with the expected delivery; and,
b. The BAC to coordinate with the MEO in the indication of PPA timelines in the Annual Procurement Plan and strictly execute all procurements according to schedule.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members immediately stop the practice of granting fund transfers as financial assistance and donations to its constituents regardless of the source of fund as this is not among its mandated duties and functions under Section 447(a) of R.A. No. 7160. Also, instead of appropriating funds for donations under the said office it should be transferred to the MSWDO who has the expertise in doing similar functions and is tasked to implement such programs, projects and activities.

In order to achieve a self-reliant municipality aspired by the Local Government Code, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or revision of the same that is effective and operate particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall direct the Mun. Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drives to boast revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management.

The Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects.

In order to achieve a self-reliant municipality aspired the Local Chief Executive shall make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or revision of the same that is effective and operate particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall direct the Mun. Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drives to boast revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management.

The Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects.

1.3. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Compute for the depreciated replacement cost of the spare parts replaced
as a result of the major repair of the ambulance; and,
b. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted.

2.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to assess the value of works associated to the minor repairs
included in the contract and the cost of restoration of the majorly damaged
cottages and furnish the Municipal Accountant the results of the same; and,
b. The Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entries to correct the
foregoing.

3.17. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Head of the Rural Health Unit and the LDRRMO to maintain records and
monitor the receipts and issuances of all supplies and properties issued to
the unit/office, and, regularly consolidate the consumption and issuances of
these items for the use of the GSO;
b. GSO to conduct semestral Report of the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) and compare this Report to the records of consolidated issuances of
the department heads for reconciliation; and,
c. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with the RPCI as basis in recognizing
related expenses.

4.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer to
determine with reasonable certainty – based on records and documents – the
identification and budget allocation for priority development projects to ensure that
the 20% development fund is fully optimized in implementing all planned projects that
the fund could possibly finance.

5.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Municipal Engineering Office in its preparation of the required PPMP, as
supported by approved Program of Works, to include the infrastructure
projects to which the office is tasked to implement to facilitate the
preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan in order that procurements
are aligned with the expected delivery; and,
b. The BAC coordinate with the MEO in the indication of PPA timelines in
the Annual Procurement Plan and strictly execute all procurements
according to schedule.

6.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget
Officer to strictly monitor disbursements from the LDRRMF to avoid non-related
activities being charged from the fund.

6.13. We also recommended that the LCE direct the LGU Driver and all personnel
concerned to properly fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket to aid the GSO and the
Municipal Accountant in the fuel consumption monitoring of the LGU.

6.14. Further, we recommended that management submit a justification explaining the
deficiencies shown in Table 13.

7.14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Direct the former and the incumbent Barangay Chairperson and the
Barangay Treasurer to locate the transaction documents related to the
purpose of the grant and to seek alternative remedies to be able to liquidate
and strictly implement proper turnover of the accounts and documents of
the barangay, inasmuch as the transactions related to the outstanding
unliquidated fund transfers are concerned, and impose appropriate
administrative and legal action for refusal to do so; and,
b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor and strictly implement
the rules underlying all financial assistance granted to the barangay in 2021
and the years thereafter as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the
stipulation of the MOA.

8.15. We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members immediately stop the practice
of granting fund transfers as financial assistance and donations to its constituents
regardless of the source of fund as this is not among its mandated duties and functions
under Section 447(a) of R.A. No. 7160. Also, instead of appropriating funds for
donations under the said office it should be transferred to the MSWDO who has the
expertise in doing similar functions and is tasked to implement such programs, projects
and activities.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Mun. Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to restore the appropriate balances of the STF and Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account that will ascertain fair presentation of the financial position under the Trust Fund account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, being concerned ensure that the Mun. Accountant to strictly observe the details in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its utilization.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoins the Mun. Budget Officer to monitor savings after application of funds to facilitate its recommendation for approval of supplemental budget on its further utilization to maintain regularity of the transactions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, being the Head of the LDRRMC, ensure that updated and/or revised LDRRMFIP is duly approved before any charges thereof to maintain regularity and validity of transactions in line with existing law and local ordinances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and other local officials concerned ensure compliance to the established guidelines on the proper accounting of relief goods used for operations/distribution to ascertain receipt thereof by the targeted beneficiaries and eventually satisfy the primary purpose of the grant in responding future similar emergency situations.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the GSO and Municipal Engineer to conduct the required valuation of the inventory of the Local road network system within the Mun. jurisdiction in order to achieve accurate and reliable presentation and disclosure of the Road Networks account of LGUs financial position.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of the Local Roads was conducted and valuation of the Local road accounts was conducted and updated except for the inclusion of the road lot component.

observations.recommendation: The management shall adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable waste for possible recycling. Also, management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Mgt. Plan and ensure effective-ness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Mgt. Program. Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to restore the
appropriate balances of the STF and Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account that
will ascertain fair presentation of the financial position under the Trust Fund
account.

2.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the required details in the Notes to Financial
Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements
over transparency and accountability of its utilization.

3.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoins the Municipal
Budget Officer to monitor savings after application of funds to facilitate its
recommendation for approval of supplemental budget on its further
utilization to maintain regularity of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make representations to the
Local Sanggunian for further closure of the remaining dormant and unnecessary
trust funds, and revert the same to the general fund to sustain its programs or projects
implementation for the benefit of its constituents, and also achieve the fair presentation
of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the GSO and Municipal
Engineer to conduct the required inventory of the local road network system within the
municipal jurisdiction in order to achieve accurate and reliable presentation and
disclosure of the Road Networks account of LGU’s financial position.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to properly classify the Breeding Stocks under Biological Asset
account and update its ledger cards to reflect the actual carrying value of the
Biological Assets in order to achieve reliable and faithful presentation of Financial
Statements. Also, enjoin the Municipal Agriculturist to initiate reconciliation of its
records with the Municipal Accountant to achieve the appropriate amount and
accounts related to Biological Assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall confer with the Municipal
Budget Officer on the strict adherence to the established guidelines on
appropriation and utilization of 20% DF.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the policies
and procedures for the release and utilization of prior period calamity fund
balances consistent with the fundamental principles on government financial
transactions and operations

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant on
the preparation of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and
enjoin the designated LDRRMO on the regular submission thereof on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month to COA Office to avoid delay in the delivery of audit
results that supports significant management decisions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to
strategize and strengthen more its RPT collection drive by extending beyond the
prescribed procedure of posting notices and take initiatives to reach directly the
taxpayer’s attention over its respective obligations over realty tax in order to realize its
goal and objective of providing more better services and facilities back to its constituents

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries to properly classify the loans as to current and long-term payables with appropriate disclosures. Also, the enhanced requirements in the Notes to Financial Statement shall strictly be observed in order to enhance the reader’s and user’s understanding of the financial results

In order to achieve a incremental self-reliant municipality aspired by the Local Government Code, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or revision of the same that is effective and operative particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drives to boast revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management
observations.managementAction: LGU to request from BLGF for new schedule of LRC updates
observations.reason: Local Revenue Code subjected for updates

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ascertain that hiring of Job Orders be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated HRMO for the screening and staffing to ascertain the capabilities and skills of hired personnel for the job at hand

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjustments on its subsidiary reports to reconcile with the controlling account in the financial statements in order to achieve its proper presentation and disclosures

The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the 1% allocation from the IRA to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter for the welfare of the nation’s future capital assets

The Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects

Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at the least cost or most effective means; and Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office

The Management shall adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, RCA and Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare seventeen (17) regularly the monthly bank reconciliation statement for its bank accounts and submit to the auditor within the reglementary period

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting
entries to properly classify the loans as to current and long-term payables with
appropriate disclosures. Also, the disclosure requirements in the Notes to Financial
Statement shall strictly be observed in order to enhance the reader’s and user’s
understanding of the financial results

In order to achieve a self-reliant municipality aspired by the Local Government
Code, we recommended that the Local Sangguniang Bayan shall initiate the amendment of
certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or the promulgation of the Market Code that
is effective and operative particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall
direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drive to boast
revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ascertain that hiring of Job
Orders be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated
HRMO for the screening and staffing to ascertain the capabilities and skills of hired
personnel for the job at hand

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary
adjustments on its subsidiary reports to reconcile with the controlling account in the
financial statements in order to achieve its proper presentation and disclosures
The Municipal Accountant shall recognize the required depreciation and possible impairment of PPE accounts to reflect its proper value and measurement in the financial statements
The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the optimum utilization and completion of its commitments set as Priority Projects funded from the 20% DF to attain the desirable outcomes of the LGU

The Local Chief Executive shall strongly advocate to the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government

The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the 1% from the IRA to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter for the welfare

The Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects

The Local Chief Executive shall intensify the collection of loan repayments by requiring the Municipal Accountant to impose the actual amount billed and for the Municipal Treasurer to consequently remit the same promptly to the GSIS. The practice of partial or non-deduction of loan amortizations from the respective payroll shall strongly be discontinued in adherence to the requirements of the GSIS Act that redounds on the benefits accorded to the active and separated personnel of the government

The Local Finance Committee shall ensure the integration of the GAD Plan and Budget in the budget proposals from different offices of the LGU during technical budget deliberations with due considerations on the guidelines and prohibitions in allocation and attribution that only GAD related PPAs shall be considered thereof so as to serve the purpose of establishing the said GAD Fund and to advance the benefits accorded to the targeted beneficiaries

The Local Chief Executive shall require the improvement of water system facility at the Municipal Health Center including the rehabilitation of the chlorination system facility to revive and sustain the purpose of the SALINTUBIG Program in providing safe access to potable water for improved health and well-being of the community

The Local Chief Executive shall create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, the LCE shall require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties

Require concerned officials to fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to (a) accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reversion or remittance of any unutilized balance of fund transfers for completed PPAs and submit liquidation reports to the source agency.

Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at the least cost or most effective means; and Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office

The Management shall direct the MPDO to conduct close monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation of barangay projects, especially those that the municipality had subsidized. A Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) which details the conditions and obligations of the parties, particularly the submission of liquidation reports, should first be executed prior to the release of assistance to Barangays

The Local Chief Executive shall require the Annual Budget includes specific priority projects identified by the Local Development Council. Management must see to it that the projects funded out from the 20% EDF are implemented as planned or scheduled

The Local Chief Executive shall direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, respectively, to : (1) transfer or credit the amount of P6,409,347.07, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2012 to 2016, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund; and (2) record such transfer by drawing up accounting entries. Also the Municipal Accountant have to (3) provide adequate disclosure of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE at year-end; and (4) maintain separate subsidiary ledger per source and year to keep track of the balances of these special trust fund within the 5-year period

The Management shall adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies

The Local Chief Executive shall require the GAD Focal Person to plan, focus and undertake definite activities which will: Enhance women’s access to all types of health care; Lower maternal mortality rates and provide adequate family planning services; Enhance the situation of women in the informal economy; Encourage women victims of violence to report their ordeal

The Management shall direct the BuB focal person/(s) to fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2015-2016 BuB Fund releases, in order to (a) accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated and resolved in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to grantor-partner agencies

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management stop immediately the practice of deducting from the payroll and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions. Follow strictly the provision of Section 48 of RA 10651 or the General Appropriation Act, FY 2015.
observations.reason: Some employees with accountable unliquidated balance did not heed the cash demand letters issued to them. Lack of intervention on the part of LCE to compel employees to settle their cash advances

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe the provisions of Section 10, R.A 9184, Section 54(1) and Item 2(a) of Annex “H” and other applicable provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation issued under GPPB Resolution No. 3-2009 dated July 22, 2009 to avoid recurrence of this observation.

We recommend that the management stop immediately the practice of payments of loan amortizations as deduction from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions. Follow strictly the provision of Section 48 of RA 10651 or the General Appropriation Act, FY 2015.

We recommend that all procurement of medicines be supported with certification by the requisitioning officer that the medicines procured were in conformity with the essential drug list in the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume I to attain the country goals of making available, safe, efficacious and quality drugs at affordable cost.

We recommend that management strictly observe the rules provided in DSWD Department Order No. 22, as amended by Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008, to ensure that the fund will be used in accordance with the intended objectives, and it will be paid to desired qualified beneficiaries.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011 dated April 13, 2011 on the use/disbursement of 20%EDF.
We recommend that the Mayor require all concerned officials to abide strictly with Section 3 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-03 dated November 9, 2007, and refund the amount of P42,000.00.

Require the Municipal Accountant the immediate submission of all financial reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, financial management should see to it that Section 7.2.1 paragraph (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of RA 7160 be strictly complied, by close coordination and follow-ups with the concerned responsible officers/employees.
observations.reason: Reports with complete supporting documents were submitted

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above-listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations. Management should impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for by the law and regulations against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.
observations.reason: The abovelisted reports were immediately submitted with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations.

observations.recommendation: Secure the interest of the LGU over its properties by ensuring them with the GSIS General Insurance Fund. Comply with RA 656 to ensure that the municipality is compensated or indemnified for any event of loss/damage to its properties.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare regularly the monthly bank reconciliation statement for its bank accounts and submit to the auditor within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Lack of Manpower at the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.

We have recommended that Management:
Require concerned officials to fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by these fund transfers in order to (a) accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reversion or remittance of any unutilized balance of fund transfers for completed PPAs and submit liquidation reports to the source agency.

We have recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at the least cost or most effective means; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MPDO to conduct close monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation of barangay projects, especially those that the municipality had subsidized. A Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and Barangays  which details the conditions and obligations of the parties, particularly the submission of accomplishment or liquidation reports, should first be executed prior to the release of assistance to Barangays.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to ensure that the Annual Budget includes specific priority projects identified by the Local Development Council.  Management must see to it that the projects funded out from the 20% EDF are implemented as planned or scheduled.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, respectively, to : (1) transfer or credit the amount of P6,409,347.07, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2012 to 2016, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund; and (2) record such transfer by drawing up the following accounting entries:
Also the Municipal Accountant have to (3) provide adequate disclosure of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE at year-end; and (4) maintain separate subsidiary ledger per source and year to keep track of the balances of these special trust fund within the 5-year period.

We have recommended that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.

We have recommended that Management direct the BuB focal person/(s) to fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2015-2016 Bub Fund releases, in order to (a) accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated and resolved in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to grantor-partner agencies.

The Local Chief Executive should create and Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should properly accounted for. Then, the Property Custodian shall prepare and update the Property Cards and he should be closely supervised by the Municipal Treasurer; and the Accounting Department shall prepare and update the Real Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards. With these, regular reconciliation of accounting and property records could be undertaken. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Section 102 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all           properties of the LGU will completely/accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
observations.reason: The Treasurer’s Office still have not prepared property cards for each category of PPE. The LCE have already issued an EO constituting an Inventory Committee but the IC have not started yet the physical count of properties.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demands were already send to those accountable officers.
observations.reason:
Some employees with unliquidated balance do not heed the demand letters issued to them. 

Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances and strictly not approved cash advances of erring officials.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe the provisions of Section 10, R.A 9184, Section 54(1) and Item 2(a) of Annex “H” and other applicable provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation issued under GPPB Resolution No. 3-2009 dated July 22, 2009 to avoid recurrence of this observation.

We recommend that the management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations as deduction from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions. Follow strictly the provision of Section 48 of RA 10651 or the General Appropriation Act, FY 2015.

We recommend that management strictly observe the rules provided in DSWD Department Order No. 22, as amended by Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008, to ensure that the fund will be used in accordance with the intended objectives, and it will be paid to desired qualified beneficiaries.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint MC No. 2011 dated April 13, 2011 on the use/disbursement of 20%EDF.
We recommend that the Mayor require all concerned officials to abide strictly with Section 3 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-03 dated November 9, 2007, and refund the amount of P42,000.00.

Require the Municipal Accountant the immediate submission of all financial reports with complete supporting documents.  Henceforth, management should see to it  that  Section 7.2.1 paragraph (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of RA 7160 be strictly complied, by close coordination and follow-ups with the concerned responsible officers/employees.
